title: Stop-during-loop double-fires re-fire + silent project.json save-loss (post-208/213)
description:
Surfaced live during a Loop-driven t2i batch (Rossifi, 2026-07-08). SCOPE NARROWED after deep investigation (2 arch-map agents + instrumented logs). Two INDEPENDENT bugs SHIPPED here; a third (display-only) split to MPI-234.

(1) STOP DOUBLE-FIRES THE LOOP [SHIPPED, live-verified]. Semantics CONFIRMED with user: Stop = 'skip THIS gen, keep looping' (loop stays armed; end it only by tapping the armed Loop button). The bug was NOT the semantics — it was that ONE Stop spawned 2-3 overlapping gens. Proven by log: cancelRunningCueJob → activeGenerations.cancel() runs the store cancel SYNCHRONOUSLY, which drains the lane + loop-re-fires a fresh gen (advancing _lanes[lane].active to the NEW job) all BEFORE cancel() returns; then the belt-and-suspenders `_onLaneDrain(lane)` at the end of cancelRunningCueJob drained that NEW job → a 2nd re-fire. FIX (generationService.js ~449): capture `_cancelledIntent = _lanes[lane].active` before cancel; run the belt-drain only `if (_lanes[lane].active === _cancelledIntent)` (the genuine stuck-lane case where the store never drained — exec.jobId never assigned → bare interrupt). Now exactly ONE re-fire per Stop (log-verified: no phantom extra gens).

(2) SILENT SAVE-LOSS / project.json DESYNC [SHIPPED, logic-verified]. Real root was NOT the genService:923 swallow (that path's sidecar DID write) — it was `persistGroups()` (projectService.js ~435) swallowing BOTH network errors AND {success:false} from POST /update-project via a bare .catch(...warn). A transient write failure mid-batch left the group in renderer memory but never on disk → stale project.json + memory-only ghost ids → projectReconciler load-meta 404s on reload. FIX: check result.success, retry 4x with backoff, ui:warning toast only if all fail. Guard test tests/persist-groups-retry.test.cjs (4 assertions).

(3) SPLIT → MPI-234 [PARKED]: after a Stop, the re-fired gen's LIVE placeholder card + preview latents + status-bar N/M don't show until it finishes (display-only; gen completes+saves fine). Ordering race in the gallery's _firstRunningEntry placeholder-ownership model. 6 point-fixes failed → needs an idempotent-reconcile rewrite. Full root + arch maps in MPI-234/research/system-map.md. User chose 'ship what works, park the rest'.

NOT MPI-225 (proven). Original card's 'loop won't stop / loopArmed not cleared' framing was WRONG — Stop must KEEP the loop armed; the defect was the double-fire, not a missing disarm.
shipped: BUG1 double-fire guard — generationService.js cancelRunningCueJob (_cancelledIntent identity check on belt-drain). LIVE-VERIFIED: one Stop = one re-fire, no phantom gens.
BUG2 persistGroups retry+toast — projectService.js. Logic-verified via tests/persist-groups-retry.test.cjs; live save-fail injection NOT run (hard to force).
verify_checklist: BUG1: arm Loop, run 2 gens, press STOP once -> exactly ONE new gen re-fires (loop continues), NO burst of 2-3 phantom gens. [DONE — user + log verified 2026-07-08]

BUG2: run a batch with project.json write failing (Media read-only mid-batch or fill disk) -> ui:warning toast after 4 retries; happy path shows NO toast + all gens register on reload (no load-meta 404s). [NOT live-tested — defensive]
wrong_attempts: 6 attempts before the split. (a) clearPendingQueue disarm — wrong, user presses Stop not Clear. (b) cancelRunningCueJob disarms loopArmed — wrong, user wants loop to KEEP going. (c-f) gallery placeholder point-fixes (payload placeholder, queue-guard, always-mount, paint-by-own-tempId) — each fixed one ordering permutation, exposed another → split to MPI-234 for a reconcile rewrite. genService double-fire guard + projectService retry are the two that stuck.
